varieties.name: Bloomsdale Long Standing
varieties.traits: Dark green, heavily savoyed/crinkled leaves; compact, erect growth habit
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, produces abundantly

varieties.name: Giant Noble (Giant Nobel)
varieties.origin: Netherlands, 1926 (Zwaan and Van der Molen); All-America Selection winner 1933
varieties.traits: Very large plants with spreading habit; triangular to spade-shaped leaves up to 38cm tall; lightly savoyed texture with prominent veining
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive with giant leaves ideal for cooking

varieties.name: Viroflay (Monstrueux de Viroflay)
varieties.origin: Paris, France, 1873 (from Epinard de Flandre breeding program)
varieties.traits: Massive leaves up to 10 inches long; semi-savoyed; fast-growing and vigorous
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yielding with monster-sized leaves
varieties.availability: True Leaf Market, West Coast Seeds, Australian seed companies

varieties.name: Matador
varieties.traits: Fast-growing annual with dark green, oval, smooth to slightly savoyed leaves
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection

varieties.name: Winter Giant
varieties.traits: Frost-hardy variety with large, dark green, slightly crinkled leaves; compact growth
varieties.yield_notes: Productive for baby leaf and full size
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection

varieties.name: Tyee
varieties.traits: Semi-savoy type with thick, oval, exceptionally dark leaves; upright growth habit produces cleaner leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Fast-growing and productive
varieties.availability: Select seed suppliers (availability may vary)

varieties.name: Regiment
varieties.traits: Fast-maturing variety with good green color
varieties.yield_notes: High yields

varieties.name: Space
varieties.traits: Smooth to slightly savoyed, medium-green leaves; upright, compact habit; dark green, round, spoon-shaped leaves held off ground
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive; high yields
varieties.availability: Johnny's Selected Seeds, High Mowing Seeds, West Coast Seeds, Burpee

varieties.name: Melody (Plymouth)
varieties.traits: Semi-savoy type with tender, dark green, lightly crinkled leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Grows quickly to large plant size
varieties.availability: Anderson's Seed & Garden, Burpee, major seed suppliers

varieties.name: Avon
varieties.traits: Medium-green color; fast-growing
varieties.yield_notes: Productive
varieties.availability: Specialty seed suppliers

varieties.name: New Zealand Spinach
varieties.origin: New Zealand, Australia, Japan, China, Taiwan; discovered by Captain Cook's voyage in 1770; first Australian vegetable cultivated in England
varieties.traits: Low, spreading growth habit reaching 30-40cm height and 100-125cm diameter; small spear-shaped leaves; drought tolerant
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive cut-and-come-again crop throughout warm season
varieties.availability: Australian Seed, Botanical Interests, Diggers Club, widely available in Australia

varieties.name: Malabar Spinach (Green)
varieties.origin: India's Malabar coast (Karnataka and tropical monsoon climates); tropical Asia; first domesticated in Southeast Asia
varieties.traits: Vigorous climbing perennial vine (grown as annual in cooler climates); soft stems with almost-succulent, dark green, thick glossy leaves larger than a hand; white flowers with green stems
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields - plants grow very quickly in warm climates

varieties.name: Malabar Spinach (Red)
varieties.traits: Ornamental climbing vine with burgundy/purple stems and leaves with pink veins; pink flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive

varieties.name: Perpetual Spinach (Spinach Beet)
varieties.traits: Type of chard with short stems and large leaf blades; baby chard variety; not true spinach but closer to silverbeet
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive; longer-lived and more bolt-resistant than traditional spinach; cut-and-come-again
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, Australian Plants Online

varieties.name: Surinam Spinach
varieties.traits: Crunchy and tangy; drought hardy; ornamental with clusters of small pink flowers
varieties.availability: Northey Street City Farm Nursery, humid subtropical climates specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Brazilian Spinach
varieties.traits: Tasty, nutritious substitute for silverbeet
varieties.availability: Northey Street City Farm Nursery, humid subtropical climates specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Ceylon Spinach
varieties.traits: Beautiful clambering vine with delicate pink flowers and dark red berries
varieties.availability: Northey Street City Farm Nursery, humid subtropical climates specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Sambung Nyawa
varieties.traits: Perennial leafy green; drought hardy with orange butterfly-attracting flowers
varieties.availability: Northey Street City Farm Nursery, humid subtropical climates specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Red Cheera (Kerala Red Amaranth) 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous to tropical monsoon climates coastal areas; collected from Nilakanta family originally from tropical monsoon climates coast (USDA 1991)
varieties.traits: Intensely colored red/purple leaves, stems, petioles; plants 5-8 feet tall at maturity; large branched tassels; reddish or maroon-purple leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive; copious small black seeds

varieties.name: Green Cheera (Pacha Cheera) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to tropical Asia; traditional tropical monsoon climates vegetable
varieties.traits: Vibrant green leaves; upright growth; feathery lime green flowers
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding; fast growing
varieties.availability: Mini's Lifestyle Store tropical monsoon climates, PKR Greens, local tropical monsoon climates markets

varieties.name: Siru Keerai (Tropical Amaranthus)
varieties.traits: Two types available
varieties.availability: PKR Greens tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Valli Cheera (Malabar Spinach) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to India's Malabar coast (Karnataka and tropical monsoon climates)
varieties.traits: Climbing vine with thick, glossy, succulent leaves; white flowers; beautiful ornamental plant
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive in warm climates
varieties.availability: Mini's Lifestyle Store tropical monsoon climates, PKR Greens, widely available in tropical monsoon climates markets

varieties.name: Agathi Cheera (Sesbania)
varieties.origin: Traditional tropical monsoon climates/South Indian; named after sage Agasthya
varieties.traits: Tree with white or red flowers (white more common); leaves, flowers, stems, roots used
varieties.availability: Mini's Lifestyle Store tropical monsoon climates (seeds), found growing wild/cultivated in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Ponnanganni Cheera (Dwarf Copperleaf)
varieties.origin: Native to Asia and Pacific Islands (possibly India to Southeast Asia)
varieties.traits: Low-growing perennial herb (30-40cm height); creeping growth; roots at nodes; grows in swampy/wet areas; two varieties - green (Naatu Ponnanganni) and red/pink (Seemai Ponnanganni)
varieties.yield_notes: Hardy and productive
varieties.availability: SeedPlex tropical monsoon climates, SR Plant Nursery, Hug A Plant, common in tropical monsoon climates markets and backyards

varieties.name: All Green
varieties.traits: Uniformly green plants; semi-erect growth
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding; can be harvested 6-7 times; produces more yield
varieties.availability: Indian seed companies, agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Jyoti
varieties.traits: Fast-growing plants
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yield; leaves ready for harvesting quickly
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural seed suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Harit
varieties.traits: Suitable for hilly areas
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural seed suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Bharati
varieties.origin: ICAR-IARI New Delhi - superior to Pusa Jyoti
varieties.traits: Pure green leaves without red pigmentation; cordate-shaped leaves 25cm long and 14cm broad
varieties.yield_notes: Higher total yield than Pusa Jyoti; flowers 20-25 days later providing longer harvest
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural seed suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Vilayati Palak
varieties.origin: ICAR-IARI New Delhi - developed by crossing Local Spinach with exotic Varginia Savoy
varieties.traits: Prickly seeded variety with completely green succulent stem and leaves; vigorous, luxuriant growth; vegetative type (both male and female)
varieties.yield_notes: Average 12 t/ha green leaves in 2-3 cuttings; 6-7 q/ha seed yield
varieties.availability: ICAR-IARI, Indian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Jobner Green
varieties.traits: Can be grown even in acidic soil
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural seed suppliers

varieties.name: Hisar Selection-23
varieties.traits: Short duration variety
varieties.yield_notes: First cutting 30 days after sowing; 6-8 cuttings at 15-day intervals

varieties.name: Punjab Green
varieties.traits: Semi-erect plant with dark shining leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Average yield 125 qtl/acre; ready for first cutting 30 days after sowing
varieties.availability: Indian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Punjab Selection
varieties.traits: Light green foliage; purple pigmentation on stem
varieties.yield_notes: Average yield 115 qtl/acre
varieties.availability: Indian seed suppliers

varieties.name: Water Spinach (Kangkong)
varieties.origin: First domesticated in Southeast Asia; ideal climatic conditions in tropical Asia
varieties.traits: Semi-aquatic perennial tropical vine; stems 2-3 meters long, rooting at nodes; hollow stems make plant buoyant; trumpet-shaped white flowers with mauve center. Two main types: Ching Quat (green stem, narrow leaves, white flowers - grown in moist soil) and Pak Quat (white stem, arrow-shaped leaves, pink flowers - grown in water)
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive; fast-growing; multiple harvests
varieties.availability: Australian Seed (shipped to India), Indian/Asian grocery stores, tropical monsoon climates markets, widely available

varieties.name: Palak Cheera (Spinacia oleracea)
varieties.origin: Persia (Iran); introduced to India centuries ago
varieties.traits: Cool-season crop; standard varieties
